summary: I wrote `open_questions.md` to my declared output path with eight numbered clarifying questions for the phase gate. Since no draft requirements were present in the workspace (input.json had no resolved inputs), I grounded the questions in the ask plus the actual codebase state: the two candidate "board" surfaces (pipelines index table vs. the show page's phase-column board) and the real state machines (Pipeline, Phase, and StepRun enums, including lease expiry and worker-role matching). The questions cover: which surfaces get the summary, how to phrase concurrent step runs, deterministic templates vs. LLM-generated text, detail level for unhealthy states (stuck/failed/lease-expired), whether to surface "no worker available", elapsed-time display vs. pure event-driven updates, when to show iteration numbers, and whether the summary is interactive — each with a stated assumed default so the pipeline can proceed if a question goes unanswered.
